svg.title: Closed-loop signal chain for one wheel
svg.desc: ROS 2 cmd_vel via micro-ROS to Teensy 4.0 PID, which outputs PWM speed plus FWD/REV to a ZBLD open-loop driver, driving a BLDC motor through a 30:1 gearbox to the wheel. An AS5040 encoder on the wheel returns quadrature A/B to the Teensy PID, which closes the speed loop; the driver does not regulate speed.
svg.text: Signal chain — one wheel (closed-loop speed control)
The Teensy PID closes the speed loop — the ZBLD driver does not.
With SW1 = OFF the driver is open-loop: it switches phase power and commutates on the
motor's Hall sensors, but has no speed feedback of its own. Speed regulation lives only in the Teensy.
